claim: Force of selection declines to ~0 after reproduction
scope_note: Foundational population-genetics result; not disputed

claim: Aging is not selected FOR but tolerated once selection weakens
scope_note: Antagonistic pleiotropy + disposable soma

claim: Human & insect mortality rises ~exponentially then plateaus late
support_ids: Carey1992 PMID1411540; Curtsinger1992 PMID1411541; Barbi2018 PMID29954979
scope_note: Barbi plateau disputed: comment PMID30262471 / response PMID30337380; plateau robust in inverts, contested in humans

claim: Naked mole-rat hazard does not rise with age (defies Gompertz)
scope_note: Comment PMID31287058 raised concerns; 2024 doubled-data reanalysis upholds

claim: Lifespan varies >1000x across species; some show negligible senescence
scope_note: Directly measured demographic trajectories

claim: Cancer defenses scale with size not predicted by cell number (Peto)
scope_note: Elephant TP53 copies; correlational

claim: Aging shows conserved molecular hallmarks
support_ids: LopezOtin2013 PMID23746838; LopezOtin2023 PMID36599349; Kennedy2014 PMID25417146
scope_note: Hallmarks are a curated catalog, not an independent causal test; open-loop drift scores C4 as EXPLAINS not predicts

claim: Caloric restriction extends lifespan
scope_note: Replicated across taxa

claim: Reduced insulin/IGF (daf-2) doubles C. elegans lifespan
scope_note: Single-gene, large effect

claim: Rapamycin (mTOR inhibition) extends mouse lifespan
scope_note: ITP, 3 sites, both sexes

claim: Acarbose/17aE2/NDGA extend male mouse lifespan
scope_note: Sex-biased; replicated

claim: NDGA acts partly via IGF1R/ERBB2 (growth signaling), not only antioxidant
scope_note: Curated MoA; reclassifies NDGA as signaling-lowering

claim: Glycine & canagliflozin extend lifespan (ITP)
scope_note: canagliflozin male-only

claim: Aspirin lifespan effect is NOT cleanly replicated
support_ids: positive Strong2008 PMID18631321 (p=0.01 males); null Miller2019 PMID30916479 (no benefit at 2 doses)
scope_note: Headline robust — drop-aspirin 7/8vs0/8 Fisher p=7e-4; aspirin-as-negative 7/9vs0/8 Fisher p=1.9e-3; both held-out p<0.01

claim: Antioxidants/redox agents FAIL to extend lifespan in ITP
scope_note: Matched protocol negatives — the key discriminator. Methylene blue: median null but +6% female MAX lifespan P=0.004 (footnoted)

claim: Metformin alone (0.1%) did NOT extend lifespan; metformin+rapamycin did
scope_note: HONEST EXCEPTION: a signaling drug that failed alone

claim: A-priori signaling-direction predicts ITP outcome (held-out 94%, perm p=4e-4)
scope_note: N=17 frozen set; small-N; direction assigned before outcome; robust to aspirin (all variants p<0.01)

claim: PPI network topology does NOT separate lifespan-extenders from failures
support_ids: string_compare.json (positive 13 nodes density0.35 max-deg8; negative 4 nodes density0.67)
scope_note: Matched-null STRING check — convergence is functional (mechanism direction), not structural (one hub)

claim: DNA-methylation age clock tracks chronological age
scope_note: Multi-tissue

claim: Partial reprogramming reverses age markers / restores function
scope_note: Function restored WITHOUT removing prior damage — key for reversibility claim; open-loop drift scores C6 as EXPLAINS (shared with epigenetic-noise theory)

claim: Epigenetic age is reset to ~0 in early embryo (ground zero)
scope_note: Clock-based inference

claim: Hyperfunction/quasi-program frames aging as continued programs
scope_note: Influential but debated framework; strongest rival to open-loop drift

claim: Free-radical/damage theory predicts antioxidants should slow aging
scope_note: This is the prediction the ITP nulls contradict

claim: Loss of epigenetic information can drive aging (ICE)
scope_note: 2024 author correction (erratum) DOI10.1016/j.cell.2024.01.049 — flagged

claim: Somatic mutation rate scales inversely with lifespan
scope_note: Cross-species correlation; connector-verified

claim: Loss of physiological resilience/recovery-rate rises with age & predicts death
support_ids: Pyrkov2021 PMID34035236; Cohen2018 PMID29939206; OldeRikkert2016 PMID26765499
scope_note: CLOSEST PRIOR ART to my prediction — credited; they show correlation/extrapolation, not causal necessity

claim: Physiological dysregulation is conserved across primates
scope_note: Supports 'one drifting control state' readout view

claim: Evolutionary theory licenses only 'reference unselected'; drift direction + damage-downstream are added posits (P1-P3)
scope_note: Separates derived step from falsifiable physiological posits

claim: Mechanism-direction coding reproduced 16/17 by an outcome-blind independent classifier; convergence stays p<0.01
scope_note: Outcome-blind reproducibility check on the mechanism labels; 8/10 vs 0/7 on independent labels, Fisher p=1.9e-3, held-out 88%

claim: Information-loss theory ALSO predicts reversible functional restoration (via clock reset/OSK)
scope_note: So plain reversibility does NOT discriminate; requires fixed-clock condition

claim: Discriminating prediction sharpened: rescue at FIXED epigenetic clock + FIXED damage
scope_note: Splits drift from lesion + information-loss camps; NOT from hyperfunction (a sibling)

claim: Mallostasis (mean-reverting set-point) and Dilman elevation model are close prior art for set-point drift
support_ids: mallostasis2023 PMID38092834; dilman1971 PMID381162; morphostasis2024 PMID38636560
scope_note: Credited as precedents; my addition is the causal ordering claim

claim: Open-loop drift scores fit 8 (codes P,E,E,E,P,E,E); C2/C3 are 'explains' not 'predicts'
scope_note: C2/C3 explain (model assumes the hazard form) rather than predict; 2 predicts (C1,C5), 5 explains, 0 contradictions
